reviews.review_text:
This is probably the best ARPG out there, maybe even the best one ever made.  

Pros: (lots of pros):  Graphics are optimal and efficient without looking dated.  There is no loading of shaders or anything redundant like that.  The mood is darn near perfect for the setting.  Its a little desolate and sometimes creepy.  The music fits completely, although after countless hours in game, I'll sometimes just turn off the sound and listen to something else while I grind.  That is simply due to repetition, but it doesn't take away from the fact that the build crafting is addictive, highly enjoyable, and practically off the charts in terms of potential. The possibilities are near-endless.  One of the aspects that I really enjoy about this game is that every build starts out the same way:  You are the same little noob with a wooden club and a shield that looks like it was made out of planks from Grandma's postapocalyptic picket fence.  Somewhere around 50 level-ups later, you realize how far away those humble beginnings begin to feel.  If you make it to level 100 before the ADHD and desire to try some new, radical build kicks in, your toon has reached incredible heights and near godlike potential.  There are nearly no limits, and no default classes that lock you into any particular set of gear, playstyle, or look, (not to mention, you can fully glam your gear for a small price, so that your character can look however you choose to make them look).  You start with nothing and then make the choices that fit what you feel like doing in the moment.  Then!--you've created a class for your toon based on what inspires you that day.

Cons:  I really just wish there was more.  I can't wait for the new content to finally drop.  One thing that is a tad irritating is the drop rate on a rare component called Manticore Eyes.  You need them, and the drop rate is really abysmal.  I hope you like the Jagged Wastes, because that is where you will spend a great deal of your time if you choose to farm them.   I have a toon I farm them with.  Not the tankiest or best dps build I've ever come up with, but she can run like the wind and drop "Apocalypse, Now!" on unsuspecting Grobles and Manticores.  Pretty fun to play, honestly.  Sometimes I'll get lucky and three or four eyes will drop in a single run, so I start feeling like a few more runs through the wastes will be worth it.  Then, I hit the desert of RNG and nothing drops for another half hour.  Then, a Nemesis spawns, and nothing kills efficiency faster than that, so its an immediate "Abort Mission!"  Nice thing is, you can just exit to the menu, hit start again, and resume farming.  No major loss.  Its really the best experience one could hope for.

This game still reminds me of the glory days when this genre was special.  Take that nostalgia to the next level, and you'll be happy you landed on Grim Dawn.  I can't recommend this game highly enough.  If you only know those other ARPGs that are out there with the big backing, the AAA franchises, or even some of the other one-offs, then you haven't really experienced how much fun can still exist in this type of game.  Grim Dawn will fix you up.

I've been putting off writing a review for Grim Dawn until I put at least 500 hours into the game, but I finally grew impatient and decided I'll write up my thoughts, even before the final expansion releases.

The short version is this: if you like ARPGs, Grim Dawn is a must play and I strongly recommend it. That being said, let me elaborate a bit.

- Expansions
This review is written under the premise you buy the game with all its expansions. The base game is good, great even, but it is lacking compared to the full experience expansions provide. They're absolutely worth their money. That being said, the base game is often on sale for very cheap prices, so you can try it out and purchase the expansions at any time later.

There is also a third expansion coming soon, which will extend both the campaign and the endgame even further.

- Condition of game maintenance
Grim Dawn is an older game, having been released in 2016. The game has, however, been receiving regular updates and has received multiple expansions, with the third and final expansion coming soon (hopefully in the next few months). Thanks to these consistent updates, the quality of life is on par with more modern releases, including a wonderful UI overhaul coming in the next version. QoL features are not locked behind the expansions.

There is also a number of mods available on the Steam Workshop, including a recreation of Diablo 2, or Grimarillion, which adds classes from other games like Diablo 3 or Titan Quest.

- Graphics and sound
Graphically GD is alright. It's a 10 year old game, so don't expect miracles, but with continued improvements to the engine the game doesn't look too dated at all. It's not the shiniest, prettiest ARPG out there, but it's still a solid looking game. Sound is alright. Attacks sound punchy (as they should), music is decent and there are some quite memorable tracks. 

- Masteries
GD is sort of a spiritual successor to Titan Quest, which pioneered the dual mastery system. Instead of picking a single class, GD lets you pick two masteries, each of which have their own skill tree, to create a unique class.

For example if you pick the Demolitionist mastery with Occultist, you end up with a Pyromancer.

This means that with 9 different masteries and many synergies between all of them there are probably hundreds of strong builds you can try. You have great freedom in how you play - want to be a dual gun wielding Reaper (Nightblade + Necromancer)? Go right ahead. It's all here - melee, retaliation (thorns), dual wielding, 2h weapons, guns, shields, spells, pets, skeletons, DoTs and way more.

Thanks to damage type conversions and powerful legendary items, an experienced player can create synergies even where there don't seem to be any at first sight, greatly adding to the game's depth. The theorycrafting potential is nearly endless.

- Devotions
While exploring the world you will run into devotion shrines, repairing them awards you devotion points which you can spend in the constellation tree. This tree mostly includes passive boosts, offensive or defensive, but certain devotions also offer additional skills, which trigger under certain circumstances.

This also encourages exploration of the game's hand-crafted world.

- Gear
Gear in Grim Dawn is great.

Items can grant you additional skills, some of which are very powerful. Damage conversions can help make (nearly) any synergy viable, and target-farmable Monster Infrequent drops can fill in holes in your build. There are some great sets, but it's not like D3 where you have to build around a set. I've made powerful builds that didn't utilize a set at all. There is a lot to farm, and at times a legendary drop immediately gives me a new build idea I rush to try out.

- Campaign
Grim Dawn is very focused on the campaign, and I think it works really well. The main quests are fine, but there are many smaller sidequests scattered around. Even after hundreds of hours I still find myself discovering new secret areas and finding small (and sometimes big!) sidequests I missed. The campaign is long and varied and offers lots of optional content, and the game's world is very enjoyable to explore.

You will probably have to play the campaign at least twice - once on normal difficulty and once on ultimate. This isn't such a huge deal, as on subsequent playthroughs you can rush through normal, get started on leveling, and then hop into ultimate for the proper (imo) experience. 

- Endgame
The endgame here isn't as fleshed out as others in the genre, but it's solid. There is the shattered realm, a place to test your build's capability as you fight your way through multiple floors of a randomly generated dungeon on a timer. Skeleton key dungeons offer roguelike-ish dungeons that reset upon your death, and Crucible is a wave-based game mode completely separate from the main game, though I don't have much experience with it.

There are also multiple superbosses, some of which are very very strong and require a minmaxed build to defeat. They are also usually hidden behind a sequence of secret sidequests, creating a buildup to the intense battle.

- Overall
It's a really, really good game. Perhaps my favorite game. GD can be as challenging or laid back as you want it. There's hundreds of hours worth of content to dig through and hundreds of builds to try. The game may be a little dated in certain aspects (combat feel, graphical fidelity) but if you can move past that it's one of the best gaming experiences out there.

One of the best ARPG's out there, the dual class system opens up almost endless build options and the massive amount of gear, components and other stuff ramps this up to silly levels, The devs listen to feedback and are really great about making this the best game in its genre and the expansions are well worth the asking price with another coming soon

I picked this up 2 weeks ago, first time, as a palate cleanse for POE2 until the next season.

This game is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ amazing, disappointed in myself for taking so long to stumble on it.

It has been getting updates and content since it's release, 10 years ago.
and TODAY they announced a brand new expansion releasing after May that is ~86% the size of the base game O__O

I'm only level 39 at the time of writing this, haven't even reached the first expansion of content, and it's been a blast!
To see MORE content for a game that released over a decade ago is a sign of love and dedication from the Dev team, and the player base itself.

Can't wait to see it ^_^
Well done Crate.     :)
The game feels like a modern Diablo 2-game with alot of QOL and other extra's.

The lore, characters voice act, music background, it's great.
Factions background, equipment, goal/purpose, make players must reconsidering it although not "that deep".
The RNG, should be loved for those who teach math (Teacher and lecturer), especially chapter statistics and probability.

The multiplayer feature sometimes unpredictable. *Based on my personal experience, when you become host, your inet good although unstable. But when you join someone game, my ping spiking to 999+.

For beginner or first time playing game like Grim Dawn, you'll adapt but it's progress will fruitful to your character. 
Reveal the maps during your exploration for easier travel later.
A totally solid, back-to-basics ARPG experience that keeps getting expanded over 10 years later. Holds up against even some of the most recent games in the genre!
Really amazing game, in the vain of Diablo. Amazing single player with loads of content. Great ARPG with very fun and interesting classes. Builds are not restrictive. A must try for any ARPG fan

So far, this is my favorite game on Steam, but keep in mind that I am an ARPG newbie. 
[h3] TL;DR This game has no drawback except that I feel like a criminal for buying the complete edition for only $50. This is seemingly thousands of hours of QUALITY exciting gameplay and I didn't even want to review it yet because 70 hours still wasn't enough for me to see everything... With a single character... On a single difficulty. NUTS! Even the story and environments get interesting. [/h3]

First, if you're like me and look at the store page and find that the scenario/setting is a bit of a turn-off for you and that's why you decide to skip this... I ultimately ended up liking it; it's just the first part that's boring environment/story-wise. By the time you hit the first expansion, you'll be shocked how much of an adventure this is. Even if you only get the base game, it's really cool how far you get to travel through the world. But get the expansions man, it only gets better.

Gameplay-wise: Insane. This game is nuts. I am still playing through everything with my initial character, but the itch to make a new character has been there since level 20 a few hours into the game. Even within just one dual-class it feels like there are 5 sub-classes. I genuinely think there's probably more than 30 fun characters you could build. You can respec easily and cheaply, so the game does encourage you to go bananas and play around, though attributes are harder to respec (need a rare item). 

I think I just need to bold this too: 
[h1] there's no hidden problem here, no "Always Online" "Microtransactions" "technical issue" "AI-produced content" "3rd party launcher" [/h1] 
I hate that that's not the norm anymore, but it is what it is. As a ARPG newbie I was reading reviews for other games and I couldn't find one that doesn't have some weird problem. This is a finished game, not bait with some fishing line - Again, it's TOO finished. But that's not the reason I love this game, it's just the reason I bought it.

Grim Dawn brings the gritty supernatural world that made Diablo and its sequal, a massive hit success.  They expanded on the concept of open character design and customization.   Allowing for dual-class setups and being able to enhance gear with buffs beyond their static descriptors is a genius move that rivals other ARPG heavy hitters like PoE.

The game, along with the expansions will keep you entertained and wanting to kill loot and explore into the wee hours of the morning.

11/10.

This game and these developers are awesome. They 100% deserve the praise for this outstanding game. I almost feel bad buying it for $5 but the DLC and supporter bundles are a great way of showing appreciation. Keep it up Crate Entertainment!

Grim Dawn is well-beloved by most fans of the ARPG genre for good reason. Highly regarded but oft left out of the conversation for some reason. If you enjoy cozy ARPGs with a surprising depth of mechanics (without PoE levels of complexity) Definitely pick this up and give it a go. I recommend Grim Dawn to any and everyone who hasn't already given it a try.

Phenomenal game, seriously. 

Grim Dawn feels like the perfect successor to Diablo 2, contains all the good bits and expands upon them even further. 
I have owned this game for a few years, but have only just now played it for the first time. I'm hooked. Having so much fun that I made a second character before even beating the game, as I was too eager to try out the other classes/skills. 
Beat the main story last night and have just started the DLC with my first character. Wrapping up the end of the main story with my second character. 
To my amazement, I have found out they are still actively developing the game and have their largest DLC releasing shortly. 

My only real complaint is the pathing, which can be quite frustrating at times. Will sometimes attack enemies on the other side of walls with no line of sight, or have difficulty navigating inside buildings and up some stairs. 

9.5/10 - I wholeheartedly recommend this game.

I can't believe I didn't know about this treasure until now.  What a wonderful game!  Much more fun than Diablo IV and for a far better price.  It feels a little like a successor to Diablo II.

Don't let the age and the graphics hold you back.  The game runs beautifully on Windows 11 and the graphics are still colorful, intense and convoy the appropriate mood.

Highly recommended.

I knew GM for years, but I've never actually played it, until now. What a great ARPG, better than poe2. Gritty, grimy, dark with great sound effects even and music. I'm having a blast and I didn't even touch the DLC content yet.
It's very nice, fun and pretty for a 10 year old game. And there's still content being made for it...

It's glorious. Inspired by old school ARPGs and you can tell. Core combat is crisp is satisfying. Loot and builds are a perfect balance of experimentation with many viable options. While set items exist, it does not fall into the Diablo 3 disaster, where sets were the main goals.
10+ years of support single player game without mictotransactions and subscription.

My only regret is discovering and getting addicted to this game just now when I work full time, and have a family. Can't really play till the grim dawn comes anymore. Works like a charm with the Legion Go S, with all the ui and control options.  Oh, did I say that the biggest and highly anticipated final DLC was just announced today, coming this July? There goes our summer vacation, I suppose

Clicked on one enemy. The entire map took that personally....
Now there are 47 monsters, 300 spell effects, and my monitor is inventing new colors just to keep up.
Amazing game. 10/10

[h2] More than a [Insert popular ARPG title here]-clone [/h2]
[h1] 11 out of 10 [/h1]
This is a game that just doesn't quit. You will be amazed at the dedication Crate Entertainment puts into this game. This game stands the test of time; it doesnt even require solid-state storage nor an eternal fiber-optics connection! There are even funny things to laugh at in this grim-dark adventure! John Bourbon sure is a funny fella.

Quite fun gameplay, but suffers from many many small UI problems that spoil the game.

The map is annoying. There is no map overlay so you're always staring at the top right corner of the screen. Your character's position isn't shown on the map. The last portal you used isn't shown on the map. Icons aren't shown on the detailed map so you can't find what you're looking for. Detail isn't shown on the coarse grained map, so you don't know exactly where there things are related to each other. There aren't enough portals, so it takes ages of running over the same wasteland to finish a quest. You can't drop markers on the map so you can't track bosses you were struggling to kill or locked doors to return to.

The inventory and skills windows are also terrible. When swapping items in and out of inventory when checking how they affect your stats, they don't swap to the same place, so you have to go and find the item again to put it back on your character. There is no button to swap items into your side stash, so manually clicking and moving over individual crafting items is a chore. Why don't crafting items  have their own inventory anyway? 

The skills have useless explanations. Are spells of similar type grouped physically together? Ha, no, they're just scattered randomly on the page because who needs usability design. Half the text is dedicated to some backstory of the spell inventor, instead of explaining what the spell actually DOES. Does the spell affect your weapon or not? Do you have to cast it or is it passive? WHO KNOWS. Go read two wikis and still be confused so make a reddit post to find out. The game sure won't tell you. Or show you. None of the status indicators of ailments have any text on the screen. Is that green stuff poison or acid? Who knows.

Then there's the astrology skills. Do you want to filter all the purple ones? TOO BAD, YOU CAN'T. Do you want to filter for green skills for mages? Nope, not possible. Just hold down the joystick over and over to highlight potential skills that might suit you and keep returning to the left to hover over the filter and hope you can memorize what the skills are once your cursor moves away.
Holds up extremely well for its age and is still getting new content
The game is a fantastic old school RPG. Definitely worth the 4 dollars. NOT WORTH 25 though.
plain and simple its a very good game with absolutely 0 ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥. Every game developer should take notes on this game but we all know they wont.

Crazy game.
Bye-bye Diablo 3, bye-bye Diablo IV.
Super maps, many options for class and mods on items.
Very clever skill tree, infinite possibilities.
game is good. is much like some of games as this games is, but this game is not them but this one of such things as they are, but not so same.
One of the BEST ARPGs EVER MADE!!!! Way more fun than any Diablo and just has thousands of combos/builds. Love it!!

This the best ARPG - H&S game I've ever played. 
I have nothing more to say that hasn't already been said.
Awesome arpg/ looter. I was searching for a diablo 2 feeling and I found it !! This game is awesome it's like poe/dialbo all the best parts.

enjoying this alot so far. love this style of game and theres plenty to learn here. havent even hit level 70 yet let alone 100 to see the end game content. highly recommend if your a fan of diablo etc.

Fantastic if you're an ARPG Lover.  Intricate build possibilities.  Loot loaded with stats and tons of customization.  It's dark, plenty of zombies to kill, and there are guns too.  It's old, but I'm a new player and I love it.

A great game to play solo or with friends. Made by an awesome developer and publisher who thus far has treated the community well and has shown great care in the development of their game. If you like Action RPGs and want something for single player or with friends without dealing with the economic/trading bloat of a fully online game like Path of Exile, this is the game for you.

I have over 1000 hours in this game few a few reasons:

firstly, there's a number of classes. Because you can craft your build with any combination of 2 (or even just raw dog 1 class) there's enough skill and equipment variation that when combined with damage conversions you could make several builds with just the same combination. 
Example: Just with shaman alone you could build for two-handed, summons (which scale on player dmg), pets (which scale with pet stats), AND around damage types like bleed, lightning, cold, and physical.
I press right click and then everything starts to burn. Perfect.
Classic action RPG, good class customization and waiting for new update
A very challenging game (especially i you decide to play on hardcore) but it is worth it. I have lost the count how many times I have died in this game.

This game is very... complicated to suggest or not suggest, it has very good points and very bad points, a more neutral rating would be more reasonable. but i'll concede and recommend

The good
-This game feels pretty good to play, progression is decent enough.. vendors "sometimes" sell useful stuff
-Mob quantity is great, on top of sub-boss quantity too
-Gear feels fairly powerful, level ups feel powerful.. talent tree is well designed
-Quests don't feel pointless and will "sometimes" give you good loot
-The world doesn't feel extremely empty(until you've cleared it) unlike most games in this category

The bad
-The balance is really... bad, i can get making a game difficult but this ain't it, sometimes getting one shot and being unable to progress after a certain point isn't amazing(maybe i'm doing it wrong? idk, i've tried alot of things)
-Boss mobs are a literal sponge of hp and damage dealing potential.. into the millions of hp and potentially one shot consistently
-No toggle overlay map because.. they feel we won't look at the world(supposedly)? we run around with the map open anyway because the mini-map is so zoomed in you can't tell where the paths are, which is arguably blocking our view more instead of a transparent map overlay
-Sometimes it feels like my class kinda does zero damage versus the hordes/bosses

I'm not exactly positive what i'm doing wrong with how i play the game, but it is what it is, it can quite enjoyable until i hit one of the walls or damage checkpoints, maybe it expects you to go back and grind on the first playthrough or something. it is not a bad game by any means.. just has very questionable choices.

Right up there with my favorite ARPGs ever made. It takes awhile to settle in, and in my opinion it doesn't fully 'click' until you've tried a few different characters with a variety of classes (in this game you choose 2 classes as a combination class for every character). But once you've seen what a handful of classes can do, your imagination starts rolling and it makes you excited at the possibilities you can try. 

The game is also massive but you progress into your general 'build' rather quickly in most cases. This gives you the opportunity to actually try your imaginary combo/build ideas and see how they pan out within 10 hours or so of playing a character from Level 1.  

Once you find something you really like, you will have a TON of game to play before you've seen all the landscapes and quests, plus there are multiple faction choices to make with exclusive rewards, so you could potentially play the same class with the same stats and have a much different experience by following alternative faction questlines. It just has a ton of content, it's fun, the spells and combat feel well tuned and punchy, the physics make enemy deaths wildly dramatic, sometimes funny, and create awesome combat visuals. Seeing a guy ragdoll flop and roll back 10 feet after getting thrown by a seismic wave makes your spells feel overwhelming and powerful. 

Whether this game is on sale or not, if you like ARPGs it's sure to give you a new world of theorycrafting, vibrant buildcrafting, cool landscapes, NPCs, and quests, and great combat to glue it all together. It's a buy for sure
I'll be honest. It's a-lot better than the current games people are flocking to. I also am not paying to playtest this game. I get the whole game.

It's not for everyone, but I enjoyed it a lot. There's a lot of things to do, my personal favorite is the sheer size of the map and content. That being said, many people get bored by the somewhat repetitive combat and feel the game is too easy. If you are somewhat of a more persistent player, one who enjoys farming, etc. I would say get it.
Nice levelling system, good if slightly repetitive gameplay.

peak game, wonderful story and great gameplay.
the classes, dual classess and builds you can cook are so so so goooood
base game is quite good, add the dlcs and you got probably a thousand hours or more, you could ignore the crucible tho, its a bit mid.
Also soon they will add another dlc, meaning more class combos and builds!!

also if you love lore, the game as of now with dlcs has like 300 notes or so plus all the conversatioons and its variations, so you will enjopy it a loooot, such a great story.
Best ARPG on the market right now. It may be 10 years old, but it is holding the test of time.

Grim Dawn feels like a game made by people who genuinely care about what they were building. The atmosphere and artwork are fantastic, the world feels dark and interesting to explore, and there are tons of hidden areas and surprisingly well thought out questlines.

I like the balance between simplicity and complexity. There is enough depth in builds, items and theorycrafting to keep things interesting. It feels like a good middle ground between D2 and PoE.

Overall it feels polished, rewarding to explore, and made with actual passion.

This is the first ARPGs I ever play and its the best. It got so many QOLs that make it easy for the player that struggled to find it. The only thing its stuck is the expansion DLCs but it often got discount with a cheap prices I guess thats wonderful. For player that like to play Hack and Slash , ARPGs I highly recommend you this game. 

Rating : 8 / 10

Diablo 4 is my first ARPG. Got tired of gameplay that seeks to exclusively pad player time and engagement. This is a real game. Bosses have patterns and actual choreography, no one shot mechanics that you can't see due to screen pollution. The classes are actually interesting and not copy paste fantasy characters. The game not only allows but encourages class blending. There is no cosmetic store. There is no battlepass. This is a game for the players, not to print as much money as possible to guarantee an executive a golden parachute. 

Art has aged nicely. Maxed out graphics at 3440x1440p and only takes 3gb of Vram. Locked in at 144fps. Game looks great. 

Blizzard aint got ♥♥♥♥ on this game.
Best ARPG. Not an online experince so pick your posion. Still fun to play with friends.
Fun game.   Wonder around and beat on things.   Collect and upgrade gear.   Wizard spells are fun though you do have to special a bit at least in the beginning for more powerful spells.
Good game and the devs aren't money hungry and immoral like Blizzard and GGG
crunchy and satisfying. looking forward to playing more when asterkarn releases.
I didnt expect to have as much fun in this game as I actually am. I just thought I would jump in and give it a shot, now I am obsessed.
holy shiz i come from diablo and poe2 and this game is soo good i actually like it more than both since its like epoch an even balance between the two
An excellent middle-ground between modern and classic arpgs

Grim Dawn was the first isometric view arpg i really got into (i played some borderlands before) and it has ruined every other isometric arpg for me because i am used to all the qol and amazing things this game does and when other games dont have those things all i wanna do is boot up grim dawn again this is the best arpg on the market and its not close

There are 4 games I have played to the completion of the main plot and then immediately done another full playthrough. Knights of the Old Republic, Bloodborne, Elden Ring and now Grim Dawn. If you're the kind of person who likes replaying games to see how different builds and character abilities work, who finds an item or something else that gets them thinking about a new character before finishing the run on the current character then this game is for you and is 100% pure crack cocaine. 

  I wasn't expecting this, I don't like arpgs generally. Diablo I liked on a LAN back when it first came out but I've not found any of the rest of the series much fun. Torchlight 1 and 2 I found dull. I think I played Path of Exile for less than 2hours? I think that Grim Dawn being a crafted world rather than randomised is one of the things that elevates it over these other tltles. 

  The main thing that elevates it is the character builds though. Dear lord the different builds. I bought this game because I had an itch to play a game where you could experiment with different stats and abilities and this came up out of a search. Thank you random forum person! You absolutely hit that nail on the head. You pick two different classes and some Salt and Sanctuary style constellation bonuses for an insane amount of variety and an astonishing amount of viable ways to build your character. Even within the individual classes there is so much variety to focus on and two different characters with the same two classes could be totally different builds. 

 Most importantly the abilities are FUN. Dual wielding pistols while chucking molotovs? Swarms of skeletons and pets? Stabby dps insanity that zooms over to one shot bosses before AOEing their friends? Charging through a huge group of enemies gibbing them all and leaving a trail of fire behind? I've not cackled this much at ludicrously fun rpg abilities since Disgaea. 

Two plays of the main plot, one of the DLC plots done, the other halfway done. 3 other characters halfway through the main plot. It's an absolute blast and I've barely scratched the surface of this amazing game. One of the best game for your buck deals out there.
Good game in general. Challenging. But I have some connection issues from time to time.

Great game. I hate isometrics usually, but this is really fun. 

Tons of different builds, fantastic loot, and solid story.

Love this game and it's lore is so cool if you pay attention! Highly reccomended and extremely replayable solo or co-op. Super impressed that the developers continue to bring out new expantsions! If you love diablo-esq games then this one is for you! Special shout-out to the art and music as well!

The best ARPG for me!

Here's why:
1. Deep, dark fantasy atmosphere.
2. Detailed character-building mechanics.
3. Beautiful and varied locations, monsters, towns, etc.
4. Simple and convenient transmogrification (illusion) system.
5. Many different skills, skill mechanics, modifiers, etc.
6. Dynamic and exciting gameplay.
7. Wonderful DLCs, especially the upcoming Fangs of Asterkarn (FoA).
8. A lot of cool, well-developed mods.
9. A vibrant and active community.

Of course, even the best games have some drawbacks:
1. Outdated engine.
2. Limited endgame content (more will come with FoA, but still not a huge amount).
3. The game's lore isn't fully explored.

This is purely my subjective view. I don't claim to be objective — I just love this game :)

An all-time great ARPG that thoroughly satisfies whether you are just looking to zone out with point-and-click combat, or tweak builds and grind gear obsessively, or nerd out on lore. The sheer variety and flexibility is off the charts and makes this several different meals of comfort food in one menu.
Fun and simple ARPG. Old school and creative. Soon to have even more content.
having fun with it so fare but would not recommend to a person new to A.R.P.G. of this style but if you played them before you will have a blast

If I could change anything it would be giving a mode that just piles through shattered realms like let the portal just open till I choose to tap out instead of giving me dialog every boss fight.  Other than that I have come over from Diablo and much enjoy the dual classing system.  Hoping that the Fangs expansion brings with it a boost to levels and some extra devotion points I am thinking like 65-80.  Still getting used to crucible and other modes but I have much enjoyed playing and leveling the two builds I have so far on vanilla GD

Jogaço, não tenho nem o que falar. Um dos melhores do gênero. 
Há bastante conteúdo e você vai ficar viciado nesse jogo incrível com o tanto de coisa que da pra fazer. 
Não vejo a hora de zerar ele com todas as classes e testar outras builds.

I'm only a few hours in and about half way through the campaign but, man, this game is awesome. As I've taken a new interest in trying isometric/looter style ARPG's. After a little research, I decided to give this one a try. This is my first top-down ARPG I've really played aside from a few hours in Path of Exile 2 (I'll probably return to that one after this game)

The consensus: what a game! The gameplay is super fun moment to moment, each new zone feels fresh and interesting, and is packed with content. The lore is actually pretty cool. I enjoy the grim dark-fantasy and tragic NPC quest lines. The only thing that's stood out to me at times is the voice acting. Some of it sounds pretty stiff and is reminiscent of RPG's from the early 2000's. Only the more important lines and characters are voice acted. The soundtrack isn't life-changing, but it is better than many. The graphics are beginning to show their age as well, but they are passable.  Overall the setting is quite immersive and I've had similar feelings exploring this world as I did with Baldur's Gate 3 and FromSoftware's games. There's lots of little secret areas and dungeons to find and the reward for poking around is adequately satisfying--usually presenting a hidden boss, a shrine, or some cool loot opportunities. 

The class system is incredibly in depth, allowing the player plenty of customization without feeling overly complex. It might take a second to grasp how to build a character towards your ideal fantasy but give it some time and you'll figure it out. It's immensely satisfying to feel your character get stronger as you unlock cool skills and items. The abilities are fun and the animations for them are real flashy (in a fun way) without being overdone. Once you've got an interesting build going, melting enemies as you walk, the game really starts kick the fun up a notch. I think I'll continue to replay the game and try new builds in the future. 

I really enjoy the cosmetic appeal of the in-game items as well. I love that you can find drippy items in game for your character. I have only purchased the base game thus far, but I've heard the first expansion allows you to use Transmog items too, which I appreciate, even if it's locked behind a DLC. 

Some additional points: the quality of life features and UI are pretty good--they're simple and intuitive enough. Looting and difficulty feels pretty balanced to me. I really appreciate that there are essentially zero MTX in this game. This especially is one of the biggest reasons I felt more confident jumping into this one, as most modern ARPG's have all the cool gear locked behind pay walls, and that really hollows out one of the most motivating features of there kinds of games to me. This is just a good "old-fashioned," feature-complete, content-packed game. Most  everything is play to win and clearly designed with that in intention. I feel the game is fairly priced and I don't mind that the DLC's are extra. I've yet to try them myself, but I plan to after I beat the base game. Moreover, I think it's awesome the devs have supported this game for so many years with continuous updates and add-ons. Very excited to try the DLC's, especially Fangs of Asterkarn! 

Do give this one a try! Regardless of it being a decade old, I think this game has enough merit to truly contend with the other big dogs out there.
Excellent game, can't wait for 2030 (so I read) when Grim Dawn 2 will be released ... excellent product, Dialbo (and Blizzard for that matter) can suck it

I finished the game in Veteran difficulty. Tried Ultimate for a few characters but never finished those, I dunno why. This game is addicting. Personally, I think it's better than Diablo IV. 

I tried a lot of builds, only a few worked out for me that I really enjoyed. Lots of flexibility and planning. I like the Shaman and Inquisitor class the most.

It's easy to turn off your brain when playing this game. You just have to plan your initial build, hope for good loot and progression will make everything an ease later on.

I also tried Hardcore, more than 10 characters. Most of my characters never got past level thirties, but my last character reached up to 56. You can't really be careless even if enemies become easy later. I want to clear the game in Hardcore, but I'm tired for now. So I'll probably come back when the next expansion releases.

TLDR: Game is addicting, Hardcore is hard. Will come back on next expansion.

Yeah, this game is a good time. I'm only 22 hours in but didn't want to stop playing at all so far. I get the sense that I'll keep wanting to play too, because of it's offline. That's actually quite nice - I can play on my own schedule. Graphics are dated, sure, but they grew on me - I actually preferred the originals to a texture mod I tried out. Being able to rotate / zoom the camera is quite nice too - it makes the environmental story telling easier to check out and enjoy, if you're into that sort of thing. Also, the Community loves the Devs and the Devs love the Community, which shows, and is a huge win. Only wish I found this one sooner.

Old graphics aside, this game is a blast. There's a ton of replayability when it comes to testing and see how far you can make or break you build. Speaking of builds in the realm of ARPG, Grim Dawn is very forgiving in allowing you to respec your character. Felt regretted that your minion builds ain't working? Go ahead to respec your skills with little costs.

Itemizations are huge in this game, you can have ton of experimentations on how to scale your characters stat sheets, on top of the dual-class system. In ARPG fashion where endgame is kind of a huge deal, I highly recommend you to get the DLCs for Forgotten Gods or Crucible, you can easily sink in a ton of hours in these replayable endgame content. 

Tons of content, love the ragdoll physics, having one of the best lores and backstories in ARPG that I've ever get into, and MOST IMPORTANTLY? This game supports OFFLINE MODE. This final point is huge, its rare to to find a fun offline ARPG game like this.

I got this game in a bundle I purchased specifically for another game. I had no expectations, and I ended up being very positively surprised. A lot of reviews are comparing this to Diablo and saying it is superior. I can only agree.

This isn't Blizzard, so there isn't the same polish and production value. But Grim Dawn is still better than the much more recent Diablo IV. It doesn't feature unnecessary and forced online multiplayer; it doesn't drown you in a clutter of useless abilities and buffs but lets you tailor your character and select the abilities that fit most with your preferences; it doesn't overwhelm you with a bazillion superfluous game mechanics while still preserving depth; it doesn't have mobs respawn all around you every few seconds, taking away any sense of progression; it doesn't scale mobs to your levels, and lets you actually feel like you're getting stronger.

Above all, it doesn't have scummy business practices trying to milk you of every penny you have. A true gem. I wish people flocked to games like this one instead of the ones that try to sell you something at every screen and try to tell you that if you're not online with other players, you can't play what is essentially a single-player game.

From a hardcore Path of Exile player, this game is an excellent ARPG. It will probably be my go-to between 2 leagues. Holy molly there's so much to discover, to theorycraft, and so much lore to read. I love it. Also the game looks gorgeous for its age with a strong D2/Bloodborne vibe.
Top 3 ARPG games for sure, please add multicore support, its using one core and dropping fps, quake 3 had multicore support why not this game?

Absolutely
This is the most engaging game of the genre about, every time I think there is a class I don't like I find something to change my mind :D totally worth a play
Great game. Been playing it off and on since shortly after release. Satisfying arpg

Played this for the first time in 2026. It's a good ARPG. Only issue is that it's so old. My 5090 has trouble running it some times, and I had to change around a few settings to prevent random 1 second freezes. Turns out a new high end computer can actually have issues running old games.
Very fun game! Good storytelling, lots of possibilities for character development, high replay ability. Would recommend to all hack and slash enthusiasts :)

For people who enjoy the Titan Quest Game mechanics would enjoy this game a lot more.  The mobs are a bit more denser and a lot less spread out.  There is not a lot of Hand holding in the game, but is forgiving enough in your Skills and Devotions.  I would just be careful before Picking a secondary class and assigning attributes points because they are not refundable once the decision has been made.  Chosing one Damage type and focus on one or two skills will take you through the content a bit more quickly, but any active skill you chose should get you to the end of the game. 

The few possible down sides are:
1) The almost all of the maps are basically static.  There may be some variance on the maps depending on the  faction you chose.  Replaying the game feels more repeative than it should.
2) Not only are the maps static, but the drops are as well. If target farming it's great, but after awhile you miss the surprize of getting something good on a random drop.
3) Even with 9 pure classes and 36 combined classes most of the builds feel the same.  There are so many skills that feel like must haves on your build selection.  The only real difference seems like you are picking the same skills all the time with some varience in the active skills.

Even with these issues I have with the game it is a very solid ARPG and I look forward to the next expansion.

The ONLY thing I would change about this game is the controls and custom enchantments. 

[b]CONTROLS[/b]
They're not fully customizable. 

I recommend playing with a controller only because the navigating is much simpler than the keyboard.
If the WASD was normal, I'd recommend the keyboard because you can assign more skills to more slots.

[b]ENCHANTMENTS[/b]
There are too many enchantments to figure out and try to stack, and if stacking even works at a certain point is anyone's guess. I would like to have had a person in the game that could customize armor/weapons/medals etc. to fit the direction you're going. I understand it could be OP at some point, but based on the health of some of these mobs, I highly doubt that'd be an issue. 

The game is great, it has a ton of customizations/paths you can take, some are overwhelming because they only vary by a small percentage which doesn't seem to make much difference and it all gets pretty hard to keep track of. That being said this is probably one of the best RPG games since WOW/DIABLO

Great ARPG with lots of classes to mix and match and a plethora of items with all kinds of effects, It's not perfect, it gets laggy, it can even crash, but even then, it's the most fun I've had with the genre and I come back to it ever so often to rack up a few more hours.
A great game with lots of content and expansions for those who love arpgs, a must own for Diablo fans, or a very good hack and slash game.
horrible graphics but the story and gameplay is absolute cinema

I liked it, but not loved. A mindless hack and slash, nothing really stood out. The skills are nice, but ended up using only 2 for the entire game. There's a lot of customization in character/skill/playstyle but by the end of the game I was glad I was done. I only played normal, not elite/ultimate and had my fill.

Game is awesome but once you get to candle district & steelcap prepare to completely hate the games stupid map layout. Its my only real issue with the game, I cant even explain how annoying the layout is.

This is my first ARPG. I completed this game on a pirated version before and it was one of the best game I've ever played, so I decided to buy the official version with all DLCs. Great gameplay with strong replayability and many build options, story's decent, love the lore and world design to bits!
This game is 100% comeback at anytime. Play it for months and then comeback for more.
Its a good game, easy to play mechanics and familiar loot upgrade cycle.
really really wish dual wielding shotguns were somehow possible but the game is good i promise.

Crazy insane scary HOW GOOD THIS GAME IS!!!
Man, my gosh.  Some games never loose their FUN.  What a blast playing this game.  I'm hooked.
SO GOOD!

Posting after my first completion of the base game.

This is an excellent ARPG. Tons of build variety, numerous factions with tiered equipment unlocks, hidden bosses, and a meaty story to keep you interested (so long as you enjoy hunting for lore logs, like me). Unlike most I have played, story areas aren't randomly generated, which means you get to know the maps a little as you return to run optional quests. I haven't touched the base game's end-game content yet, but I definitely enjoyed my first 40 hours enough to pick up the DLC and roll a new character. 

I loved their approach to crafting. Rather than gamble endlessly at the blacksmith, you can work to upgrade gear with decent rolls by applying a few different types of augments, more of which unlock as you find recipes and level up with the different factions. Each equipment slot has its own range of potential augments, meaning you don't just express your build by assigning skill points at level up. Coming from POE, I found this approach to crafting a lot less stressful and grindy.

Awesome game. Played through multiple times. It has some weaknesses and annoyances, but overall I like it. 

Titan Quest 1 is still better IMO :P

If you liked Diablo 2 and wanted something even better: this is it.
The balance of this game is simply breathtaking! So far, any build I have tried was able to blast through the campaign on all difficulties. Even during late game most play feels rewarding and offers the perfect blend of grind and maximizing numbers game.

The environments are beautiful, too. They tell the story of the Grim Dawn very well - which brings me to the weakest part of the game: if you are in it for the dialogues, writing or cinematics, you might not get your fix. But, to be honest, the dialogues and characters are not that important after the first playthrough. 

Highly recommending this!

If you enjoy Diablo 3 style games then this won't disappoint. lots of similarities yet different in its own unique way. I would even say its a bit easier relaxed play style. I am glad I discovered this little gem of a game. Hopefully it will be as good at end game! There are lots of in depth reviews on you-tube as well as build videos if you need to know more.

In my opinion the best value and over all most fun diablo 2 clone to date.

This games weakness is its strength. It is worse than diablo 2 only in that things feel less hand picked and specific with over cluttered lore and enemies with sprawling heaps of things to discover... And though this doesn't make as refined of a game experience, there are storms of lore beaches of items as numerous as grains of sand and tons to discover.

Not a perfect game, but A great clone with lots of love attention and personal touches that make it distinct from the ooze that birthed it. Some good ooze ahh yes.

Didn't click for me. I can tell this game has been made with love for the genre, but I found the skill system janky, and the story anticlimatic.

I "completed" the game (got the congratulatory accolade and ran out of emergencies), but what are the Aetherials? The Chthonians? How did my character recover from being "Taken"? After 30ish hours, none of the game enigmas have received any meaningful progression. I expected I would help research a "cure" for the posessed and free key people to reclaim the land, but I got embroiled in another crisis instead, which is fine except, at the end of it, it feels like everything has been left hanging without resolution.

As for the skill system, I cruised through with an arcanist dumping all my points in magic massile (and passives). I tried a few other spell skills, but none felt particularly compelling. I didn't understand the point of a second class; I saw no obvious synergy.
get this over microtransaction hell predatory cash grab soulless diablo 4

It has it's moments, but when the pre-boss boss fight is harder than the actual boss... yeah...
Also, the one-hit kill mechanics of some of the pre-bosses, plus them recovering health on every PC death makes the passions fade.
Did I have fun? Mostly.
Will I play again? Highly doubt it.

This game sucks... it's broken in so many ways.... inconsistent difficulty... generally the game feels too easy and then suddenly the instant kill crystals... latency issues... one of my Soldier abilities could fire multiple times cooldown be damned and I think this is latency related since it didn't happen in Singleplayer... gameplay overall is literally just press buttons I guess... we fought the dummy at Homestead and it was the exact same difficulty as the 'final boss'... which is to say... we all just pressed a sequence of buttons based on cooldown time and the enemy eventually died...

Amazing, havnt steped foot into an ARPG snce D3 on release and while it took 5 hours for me to get the "vibe" of Grim Dawn - now im hooked. 43 hours in and im only a level 47 / 100 tank, if you have 1 hour or 4 hours to kill this keeps me coming back. the mind boggles at how many builds i want to try out and this is all before end game which i expect to add another layer to an already amazingly deep game.
dont get this game if you are looking for a hollow surface level ARPG.. this is not it. 
you will lose hours! :)
I dont know of a better single player arpg with such amazing continued support. If you claim to like arpgs you need this one.
